posts.author: karanshah137
posts.community: Data Center Value Chain in India: Investment Opportunities
posts.text: Yes, data centers located in extremely water-stressed areas might face higher local litigation, similar to what we’ve seen with beverage bottling plants in the past. We soon might see a forced shift from evaporative cooling (which consumes massive amounts of water) to liquid cooling or closed-loop systems. These are more expensive CAPEX-wise but essential for long-term sustainability. And governments might eventually mandate the use of recycled/treated sewage water for cooling to protect the water scarcity issue. The winners will likely be those who Make Green DC in Zero Liquid Discharge and R…

posts.author: meekinvestor
posts.community: Data Center Value Chain in India: Investment Opportunities
posts.text: We are again faced with a genuine environment, basic needs vs development issue. We have no data on how much percentage of water do the poor get. Seeing the lines on the taps, it doesn’t seem they get much. I am pretty clear, once water shortage is there, it is those taps that will go dry first.

posts.author: karanshah137
posts.community: Data Center Value Chain in India: Investment Opportunities
posts.text: Log in using email and sign up for their newsletter; then it will work if you want to read the article. Other names are ABB, Hitachi Energy, and L&T. I don’t agree with Railtel because purely governmental red tapeism is involved. And they will mostly rely on the railways’ budget. But if they keep doing partnerships as they did with Techno Electric and keep getting international orders, things could change. Nothing’s wrong business-wise; the company is debt-free. Good ROE & ROCE, but I personally avoid PSUs

posts.author: karanshah137
posts.community: Data Center Value Chain in India: Investment Opportunities
posts.text: image 679×400 76.2 KB Power infrastructure is an emerging structural theme with strong demand visibility across the value chain. Not a typical short-term upcycle, but a multi-year capacity-building cycle due to high demand of HVDC corridors, renewables, data centers & AI power demand, and grid modernization.
